lesson.menu_title: Talking About Your Job — IT Manager at Centauro/Nike
lesson.menu_desc: Describe your role and responsibilities and build a clear job pitch — I oversee, I lead, we distribute.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 3 — Talking About Your Job: Role & Responsibilities
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 3 -- Talking About Your Job

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, here is a quick tip about talking about your job. When someone asks what you do, do not just say your title. Tell them three things. First, your role. I am an IT Manager. Second, what the company does. We distribute Nike and Centauro products in Brazil. And third, your main responsibility. I oversee all technology projects, and I lead a team of twelve people. Use simple, strong verbs. I oversee, I lead, I manage, we implement, we distribute. Notice the present simple, because this is true every day. I lead, not I am leading. And keep it short. A good job pitch is about thirty seconds. You set the scene, you say what you do, and you stop. Try it now. Say your role, your company, and one responsibility, in three clear sentences. Talk soon, Daniela.

Hi, my name is Marcus, and I work in retail technology too. Let me tell you a bit about what I do. I am a Systems Manager at a sportswear distributor in the United States. My company distributes running shoes and gym wear to stores across the country. My main responsibility is the IT infrastructure, so I oversee the servers, the network, and the store systems. I also lead a small team of five engineers, and we implement new software for the warehouses. The retail world moves fast, so every day is different. Sometimes I am in meetings, and sometimes I am fixing an urgent problem. I really enjoy it. It is great to meet another IT Manager who works in the same industry. So, Daniela, tell me, what do you do?
